{"key_event":"Italian militarized colonial consolidation and Ethiopia war","reasoning":"Italian Somaliland saw a major rise in administrative, coercive, infrastructural, and economic control, though British Somaliland remained a thin indirect-rule state and pastoral interiors were still not fully penetrated. Civil society weakened as fascist rule, plantation land control, recruitment, racial segregation, and wartime controls reduced autonomous clan and associational space, even while clan structures retained some leverage in the British north and rural areas.","society_change":-0.8,"society_power":3.7,"state_change":1.1,"state_power":5.6}
